source.definition: A celestial object consisting of a nucleus of ice and dust and, when near the sun, a glowing tail of gas and dust particles.

explanation.sourceText: The English word 'comet' comes from the Medieval Latin 'cometa', which in turn derives from the Ancient Greek 'komētēs' (Κομήτης), meaning 'long-haired', referring to the glowing tail of the celestial body.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „comet” provine din latina medievală 'cometa', care la rândul său derivă din greaca veche 'komētēs' (Κομήτης), însemnând 'cel cu păr lung', referindu-se la coada luminoasă a corpului ceresc.

target.definition: Un corp ceresc cu o coadă luminoasă care orbitează în jurul soarelui.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word 'cometă' comes from Latin 'cometa', borrowed from Greek 'komētēs', meaning 'long-haired', referring to the tail of the comet.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „cometă” provine din latină 'cometa', împrumutat din greaca veche 'komētēs', care înseamnă 'cel cu păr lung', referindu-se la coada cometei.

examples.sourceText: The comet is visible in the night sky.
examples.targetText: Cometa este vizibilă pe cerul nopții.

examples.sourceText: The famous Halley's comet appears approximately every 76 years.
examples.targetText: Celebrul cometă Halley apare aproximativ la fiecare 76 de ani.

examples.sourceText: Scientists closely observe the comet's orbit.
examples.targetText: Oamenii de știință urmăresc cu atenție traiectoria cometei.

examples.sourceText: A comet often has a glowing tail.
examples.targetText: O cometă are adesea o coadă luminoasă.

learningTips.text: The English word 'comet' sounds similar to the Romanian 'cometă'—both come from a word meaning 'long-haired', referring to the comet’s glowing tail.
Remember the comet’s bright tail as a long strand of hair in the sky—this helps you recall the Romanian word 'cometă'.
Think of a comet as a celestial traveler with flowing hair, glowing as it moves—'comet' and 'cometă' share this image.
When you hear 'comet', imagine a shining hair strand streaking across the night sky—that image links easily to 'cometă'.
